changes_made: Renamed k8s_rbac_change_detection_enabled to k8s_rbac_unauthorized_change_detection for clarity
review_notes: The functions are generally well-mapped to Kubernetes security controls related to detecting and responding to unauthorized changes. However, the naming of the RBAC function was slightly unclear and has been adjusted for better specificity. The functions align with the control's intent to monitor and respond to unauthorized configuration changes.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly indicate the specific unauthorized change detection mechanism.
title: Configuration Settings - Configuration settings | respond to unauthorized changes take the following acti
step1_reasoning: The control CM-6(2) focuses on responding to unauthorized changes in configuration settings. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to several areas: using admission controllers to detect and prevent unauthorized changes, leveraging audit logs to alert on unauthorized changes, ensuring RBAC policies are in place to detect unauthorized role changes, and using etcd integrity checks to ensure configuration data has not been tampered with.
